language_stack.framework: Plain stdlib + http.server-style statusd on 127.0.0.1:8765; MediaPipe + OpenCV + pyobjc for opt-in webcam eye tracker; stdio MCP server under src/mcp/
language_stack.deployment_target: macOS-only for Swift surface and LaunchAgent lifecycle; Linux CI runs Python unit suite + loopback backend smoke

hygiene_evidence.LICENSE: LICENSE (1068B, MIT) + LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE (dual-licensed, added in 6cf1cf5)
hygiene_evidence.README: README.md (10028B, components, quick install, CLI lifecycle, privacy boundary, runtime URLs)
hygiene_evidence.CHANGELOG: CHANGELOG.md (399B, Keep-a-Changelog with Unreleased section; empty placeholders await first release entry)
hygiene_evidence.EDITORCONFIG: .editorconfig (214B, root=true, space indent, lf, utf-8, final newline, *.toml override)
hygiene_evidence.CI: .github/workflows/ (6 workflows: ci.yml with quality-gates + package-metadata + unit-tests + backend-smoke; codeql.yml; journey-gate.yml; scorecard.yml; secrets-scan.yml; trufflehog.yml)
hygiene_evidence.CONTRIBUTING: CONTRIBUTING.md (1251B, workflow + validation + privacy checklist forbidding raw frames, screenshots, landmarks, embeddings, gaze, keystrokes, audio)

dup_exposure: phenotype-bus / phenotype-event-bus (typed local event bus is the explicit long-term target per docs/architecture/LONG_TERM_PLAN.md; replacing the ad-hoc HTTP /dev/eye + /dev/state + /event fan-out would consolidate inter-module signals)
phenotype-request-id (request ID propagation on the loopback statusd at 127.0.0.1:8765; would let the menu-bar app and CLI correlate logs across processes)

phenotype-journeys (journey tracing primitives — CI already has .github/workflows/journey-gate.yml but no source-side journey emitter; could share the org journey vocabulary with the live session/event surface)
phenotype-registry (service registry for naming the statusd/mcp/monitor processes in journeys and observability payloads)
phenotype-shared / phenotype-tooling (shared Justfile/Taskfile/CI recipes — repo's Justfile is hand-rolled; the phenotype.just library extended variant is the org baseline)
phenotype-dep-guard (dependency policy; Python equivalent (pip-audit, safety) could share the org baseline; not yet wired in CI)
phenotype-postfx (post-effect / middleware runner for the boot CLI install/uninstall/doctor pipeline; currently each step is hand-orchestrated in scripts/)
phenotype-omlx (no clear overlap; flagged for review since both target local-first ML/mediapipe-y workloads on macOS)

phenotype-voxel / phenotype-terrain / phenotype-water (no current overlap; flagged for review only because the floating gaze dot UI could share voxel/terrain rendering primitives if the Swift monitor ever moves to a 3D workspace overlay)
sota_gaps: SSOT is a stub — docs/SSOT.md admits 'Hexagonal: in progress', 'Ports: N/A', 'Adapters: N/A', 'Domain: N/A'; needs a real architecture SSOT or removal in favor of the parent phenotype org SSOT
No coverage tooling — no coverage.py, no coverage-badge, no [tool.coverage.*] in pyproject.toml, no xml/html report; only ruff + pyright + pytest in CI
No pip-audit / safety / SBOM step — dependabot covers updates but no vulnerability scan in CI (no equivalent of cargo-audit for Python)
No yamllint for the 6 GitHub workflow files and packaging/*.yml — risk of drift given the heavy workflow-hygiene churn visible in branch list
No Swift lint/format — src/native/macos/*.swift (12 files) has no swift-format, no SwiftLint, no Xcode build in CI; the entire native UI surface is un-linted and un-built by automation
No macOS CI runner — only ubuntu-latest across all 6 workflows; the Swift monitor build and the install.sh launchd path are never exercised by CI
No [project.optional-dependencies].dev — pyproject.toml declares `eye` extras but no `dev` extras; CI installs pytest/ruff/pyright/numpy ad-hoc per step instead of `pip install -e .[dev]`

No pytest plugins — no pytest-cov, no pytest-xdist, no syrupy/snapshot, no pytest-mock, no pytest-asyncio; CLI flag-parser tests, gaze smoothing, and projection-hold recovery gate are all asserted by hand
Pyright is configured 'basic' (typeCheckingMode = 'basic') — no strict mode, no mypy, no incremental type-check gate
AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md point at phenotype-dep-guard-style rules (no raw frames, no screenshots) but there is no automated redaction check in CI to enforce them on PRs
No release/publish workflow — no .github/workflows/release*.yml, no version bump automation, no PyPI trusted publishing, no signed tag flow (the 47-commit history has no annotated tags)
No SBOM / no CycloneDX / no in-toto attestation — only secrets-scan (trufflehog) + scorecard; supply-chain attestations are absent
Justfile and Taskfile.yml are duplicated — both already do the same polyglot-detect → install/build/test/lint dispatch; one should be removed or the other downgraded to a thin wrapper

Renovate config is just `{ extends: ['config:base'] }` (32B) — no grouping, no schedule pinning, no automerge, no php/js ecosystem rules despite this being a Python repo; functionally a no-op given dependabot.yml already exists
No pkg/dist build artifact verification in CI for the macOS .pkg builder in packaging/scripts/build-macos-pkg.sh — only `bash -n` syntax check + `--dry-run`
convergence_rationale: Active, locally-installed macOS runtime (LaunchAgent-managed daemon + Swift menu-bar monitor + opt-in webcam eye tracker with a 9-point calibration) — not a library, not a web app. Production weight is concentrated in the loopback HTTP statusd, the stdio MCP server, and the Swift UI surface; the design goal is a real local event runtime that correlates input/output/env/agent signals. Best fits 'live' cluster: it has a live install under ~/.local, a live install script (scripts/install.sh), and a long-term plan to expand into native collectors per OS.

action_items.title: Add macOS CI runner + Swift build/test/lint for src/native/macos/
action_items.why: Twelve Swift files define the entire user-visible menu-bar surface (gaze dot, status panel, calibration eval) and are never compiled in CI. The macOS-24.04 equivalent path needs swift-format, swift build, and an XCTest target for CalibrationEvalStats/VisualGazeFilter/MonitorUIStateStore so the projection-hold recovery gate changes (recent commit 29a03fa) ship with a regression test.

action_items.title: Add coverage, pip-audit, yamllint to CI quality-gates
action_items.why: Closes the largest SOTA gaps in one PR: zero coverage tooling, zero Python vuln scanning, zero YAML lint. Adds coverage.py + pytest-cov, pip-audit (or `uv pip audit`), and yamllint with a .yamllint.yml baseline. Targets the 6 workflow files and packaging/*.yml that have been churned heavily (chore/workflow-hygiene-* branches visible).

action_items.title: Promote Justfile to consume phenotype.just library extended variant and remove Taskfile.yml duplication
action_items.why: Justfile (73 lines) and Taskfile.yml (183 lines) both implement the same polyglot-detect → install/build/test/lint/clean dispatch. Consolidating on the org Justfile + phenotype.just library (already used by PhenoObservability) eliminates ~150 lines and aligns with the fleet baseline adopted in 73c7b74.

action_items.title: Replace SSOT stub with a real architecture SSOT (or link to parent phenotype SSOT)
action_items.why: docs/SSOT.md:17-20 self-reports 'Hexagonal: in progress' / 'Ports: N/A' / 'Adapters: N/A' / 'Domain: N/A'. Either fill it in (long-term plan + current collectors + statusd HTTP surface as adapters) or replace it with a pointer to the parent phenotype org SSOT. Required before L2 tasks can depend on agent-user-status's hexagonal boundaries.

action_items.title: Add `[project.optional-dependencies].dev` and migrate CI to `pip install -e .[dev,eye]`
action_items.why: pyproject.toml already has the `eye` extras group; adding a `dev` group (pytest, pytest-cov, ruff, pyright, mypy, syrupy, pip-audit, yamllint) and switching all 4 jobs in ci.yml to `pip install -e .[dev]` removes per-job ad-hoc pip install lines and makes local `pip install -e .[dev,eye]` a one-shot bootstrap matching the install.sh contract.
